cases.title: Retrieve transaction history for existing user with default pagination
cases.summary: A GET request for a valid userId with no explicit limit or offset returns HTTP 200 with the default page of transactions.
cases.expectedOutcome: Response contains the userId and a list of transactions using the default limit of 20 and offset of 0.

steps.summary: Create a user to obtain a valid userId.
Deposit funds to create at least one transaction.
Retrieve transaction history without specifying limit or offset.

cases.title: Retrieve transaction history with explicit valid limit and offset
cases.summary: A GET request specifying a valid limit and offset returns HTTP 200 with the correct page of results.
cases.expectedOutcome: Response contains the requested page of transactions according to the provided limit and offset.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Deposit funds to generate transactions.
Retrieve transactions with limit=10 and offset=0.

cases.title: Retrieve transaction history with limit at maximum boundary (100)
cases.summary: A GET request with limit=100 is at the documented maximum and returns HTTP 200.
cases.expectedOutcome: Response returns up to 100 transactions without error.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Retrieve transactions with limit=100.

cases.title: Retrieve transaction history for user with no transactions
cases.summary: A GET request for a valid userId that has no transactions returns HTTP 200 with an empty list.
cases.expectedOutcome: Response contains the userId and an empty transactions list.

steps.summary: Create a user without any subsequent deposits or transfers.
Retrieve the transaction history for the user with no transactions.

cases.title: Retrieve transaction history with offset beyond available records
cases.summary: A GET request with an offset larger than the total number of transactions returns HTTP 200 with an empty list.
cases.expectedOutcome: Response contains an empty transactions list; no error is raised.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Deposit to create one transaction.
Retrieve transactions with offset=1000, exceeding the number of records.

cases.title: Reject transaction history request with limit exceeding maximum (101)
cases.summary: A GET request with limit=101 exceeds the documented maximum and returns HTTP 400.
cases.expectedOutcome: Request is rejected with an error indicating the limit parameter is invalid.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Attempt to retrieve transactions with limit=101.

cases.title: Reject transaction history request with negative limit
cases.summary: A GET request with a negative limit is invalid and returns HTTP 400.
cases.expectedOutcome: Request is rejected with an error indicating the limit parameter is invalid.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Attempt to retrieve transactions with a negative limit value.

cases.title: Reject transaction history request with negative offset
cases.summary: A GET request with a negative offset is invalid and returns HTTP 400.
cases.expectedOutcome: Request is rejected with an error indicating the offset parameter is invalid.

steps.summary: Create a user.
Attempt to retrieve transactions with a negative offset value.

cases.title: Reject transaction history request for non-existent user
cases.summary: A GET request for a userId that does not exist returns HTTP 404.
cases.expectedOutcome: Request is rejected with an error indicating the user was not found.

steps.summary: Attempt to retrieve transaction history for a userId that has not been created.
